name: Shrimp in Lobster Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel, devein, and butterfly the shrimp by cutting through the back about 3/4 of the way without cutting all the way through; remove the black vein. Set aside.
2. Heat wok until smoking, add oil, and put all shrimp in at once. Stir fry, keeping them in motion continuously so they cook evenly. When they begin to curl, remove and set aside.
3. Soak black beans, rinse, and mash them.
4. Heat wok, add 2 tablespoons oil, and add beans and garlic. Stir until aroma is strong, then add meat in small pieces. Stir fry until cooked for about 4 minutes.
5. Add broth, soy, and sugar. Stir until it comes to a boil.
6. Thicken the mixture with cornstarch paste gradually; do not let it get too thick. It should be like a cream sauce.
7. Slowly stir in beaten eggs. Cook for one minute.
8. Add shrimp. Mix well and serve.
Note: Fermented black beans have a unique flavor. Do not substitute with any other black bean mixture as it will change the taste quite a bit. If you do, lots of luck. FBB are small, black, dry, and very salty. Find them in Oriental groceries or sometimes supermarkets. Keep on stir-frying, Marty from MARTY FEINS (BGNJ11A).
